subcommands.dry-run: list tags that would be deleted; emit __IMAGE_TAG_GC__ sentinel with mode=dry-run
subcommands.prune: actually delete tags via docker image rm; emit sentinel with mode=prune
subcommands.keep: append tag name to pin file (~/.hx/runner-tag-pins) so it is never deleted

keep_subcommand_test.verdict: pin correctly excludes tag from delete-set

prune_on_mock_subset.method: tagged alpine:3.18 as hexa-runner:mock_old1 / mock_old2; pinned all real tags first
prune_on_mock_subset.verdict: prune path emits correct sentinel; only mock tags removed

current_state.reason_no_prune: only 1 evictable tag, below threshold of 5; per ACTION step 7 no prior approval signal

automation_recommendation.primary: pre-build hook in hexa runner Dockerfile build script: invoke `bash /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/tool/image_tag_gc.sh prune` after each successful image build to keep disk bounded at ~4 tags worth (~580MB max)
automation_recommendation.safety_note: always run dry-run first when introducing a new pin pattern; sentinel line is grep-friendly for atlas ingestion
